vectors.notes: Real mainnet Top Shot Moment 40105574 at holder 0x0bb3b2a249ca6822 (Set 90, serial 1112 of 16000, Sam Hauser 3 Pointer). Fixture captures the live Flow REST response + the raw IPFS block. A third party MAY re-run live with `OPO_LIVE=1 node conformance/run.js adapters/flow-topshot/verify.js`; the recorded fixture SHALL round-trip offline.
Synthetic derivative of pass-1 with serial patched to 99999 (> edition_size 16000). Exercises SPEC §4 step 2.
Real Flow response + IPFS raw block with a single trailing byte appended. sha256 no longer matches the multihash digest declared by CIDv0 QmQbn…smKR — exercises SPEC §4 step 3.

Real mainnet Azuki #9999 at 0x4b0207e11661e41b091697980b3d49bd59358d7c. tokenURI returns `ipfs://QmZcH4Yv…3u4/9999` (directory + path); the adapter resolves the leaf metadata CID `Qme4i1j…Fps`, hashes the raw block against that CID, extracts `image: ipfs://QmYDvP…1hg/9999.png`, resolves the image root CID `QmfQbR…6Kr`, and hashes that block. Live re-run: `OPO_LIVE=1 node conformance/run.js adapters/erc721-generic/verify.js --vector erc721-azuki-9999-pass`.

Real RPC + metadata fixture for Azuki #9999 with a single 0xFF byte appended to the image root block `QmfQbR…6Kr`. sha256 no longer matches the declared multihash digest — exercises SPEC §4 step 3 for a path-resolved media leaf.

Two-of-two gateway agreement. Primary gateway gateway.pinata.cloud (Pinata Cloud, Inc.) and independent second gateway ipfs.io (Interplanetary Shipyard) both advertise the same leaf CIDs for Azuki #9999's metadata and image paths. Probed live 2026-04-23: gateway.pinata.cloud returns the leaf CID via `etag: "<cid>.raw"` on path-style GET (200 + raw bytes); ipfs.io returns `x-ipfs-roots: QmZcH4YvBVVRJtdn4RdbaqgspFU8gH6P9vomDpBVpAL3u4,Qme4i1jbJvY8mfDWfFXLKh1ZLy1WtKh4edHXdFWfA61Fps` via HEAD (GET 406s through its trustless-gateway.link redirect). The secondary is probed with HEAD because the cross-check only needs the header-advertised leaf CID, not bytes. Exercises SPEC v0.4 §5 trust-assumption strengthening.

Real mainnet Pudgy Penguins #1 (contract 0xBd3531dA5CF5857e7CfAA92426877b022e612cf8, 8888 supply). Exercises encoding combinations that Azuki does not: (a) CIDv1 base32 baseURI `ipfs://bafybeibc5.../1`; (b) CIDv1 raw-codec metadata leaf `bafkrei...` (multibase `b`, codec 0x55); (c) two-segment path `penguin/1.png` for the image ref; (d) chunked-file image root block of 104 bytes whose Merkle links, not inline UnixFS, anchor the PNG data. The same erc721-generic adapter that verified Azuki #9999 verifies this vector with no collection-specific code. Live re-run: `OPO_LIVE=1 node conformance/run.js adapters/erc721-generic/verify.js --vector erc721-pudgy-1-pass`.

Two-of-two gateway agreement for Pudgy Penguins #1. gateway.pinata.cloud (Pinata Cloud, Inc., primary, GET raw block) and ipfs.io (Interplanetary Shipyard, secondary, HEAD) both advertise `bafkreiclss3...` for `bafybeibc5.../1` AND `Qma6fcCG...` for `QmNf1.../penguin/1.png`. Probed live 2026-04-23; both gateways returned identical leaf CIDs via etag (Pinata) and x-ipfs-roots (ipfs.io). Exercises SPEC §4 step 3 cross-check branch on a DIFFERENT collection, path shape, and leaf CID version than the Azuki crosscheck vector.

Real RPC + metadata fixture for Pudgy Penguins #1 with a single 0xFF byte appended to the image root block `Qma6fcCG...`. sha256 no longer matches the declared CIDv0 multihash digest — exercises SPEC §4 step 3 on a dag-pb root-block (chunked file) rather than a UnixFS-inline block as in Azuki. The integrity guarantee is the same: a single byte of divergence in the root block changes its sha256 and causes step 3 to fail.

Synthetic fixture where the second gateway returns a different leaf CID for the tokenURI directory path. Models a substitution attack scenario: a compromised primary gateway returns bytes that hash to a substituted leaf CID; the independent second gateway's path resolution disagrees, and the verifier fails step 3 on `crosscheck mismatch`. Exercises SPEC v0.4 §4 step 3 crosscheck branch.

Real mainnet FXHASH GENTK token 1 (`FXHASH Generative Logo #2`, artist ciphrd, minted 2021-11). First Tezos FA2 conformance vector: verifies against Tezos native RPC (no indexer) at mainnet.api.tez.ie (ECAD Labs). The adapter does NOT call tzkt.io. Two chain reads: (a) ledger big-map 22785 at script_expr_hash(pack(Pair tz1PoDdN..., 1)) = `exprvF4Mr36o3tTwvjYUkXxEaCrChqBJL86DAXRoYjn4dzTyqXDXzE` — returns `{int:"1"}` (balance=1 confirms holder); (b) token_metadata big-map 22789 at script_expr_hash(pack(nat 1)) = `expru2dKqDfZG8hu4wNGkiyunvq2hdSKuVYtcKta7BWP6Q18oNxKjS` — returns Pair(1, {"": 0x697066733a2f2f516d...}) whose bytes decode to `ipfs://QmagGP...tbY`. The manifest's `artifactUri` resolves to `ipfs://QmavLK...L4d`. Both CIDv0 blocks fetched from gateway.pinata.cloud and hashed sha2-256 against their multihash digests. Live re-run: `OPO_LIVE=1 node conformance/run.js adapters/tezos-fa2/verify.js --vector tezos-fxhash-gentk-1-pass`. Cross-chain significance: this is the first adapter exercising a chain whose ownership model (FA2 ledger keyed by (address, token_id)) lacks a `ownerOf(token_id)` primitive — the claimed holder MUST be supplied as input and then confirmed against the ledger. See SPEC §5.3.

Real Tezos burn-address ledger lookup: script_expr_hash(pack(Pair tz1burn..., 1)) = `exprtky4jfp5t4NSVwHZpU9p1qVipJ33YC3d69EfGkXXkcp79dTYVd`; the Tezos RPC returns 404 at /context/big_maps/22785/<hash>, which the adapter treats as balance=0 per SPEC §4 step 1. The burn address does NOT hold fxhash gentk #1; the adapter fails step 1 with `holder_has_balance`. Exercises the new §5.3 claimed-holder confirmation semantics introduced for FA2-style chains.

Real ledger + token_metadata fixtures (chain reads pass step 1), but the IPFS raw block for the manifest CID `QmagGP...tbY` has had a single 0xFF byte appended. sha256 of the tampered bytes no longer matches the CIDv0 multihash digest; adapter fails step 3 on metadata_cid_hash. Demonstrates that SPEC §4 step 3 catches byte-level substitution equally on Tezos's manifest path as on Flow's and Ethereum's.
